feedback.text: To make a long story short there's enormous issues on the horizon regarding digital ownership and digital consumer protection in regards to both services and products, and it will get worse as time goes by. One of the currently most talked about issues is Stop Killing Games and how game companies are trying to shut it down before it's even off the ground properly. Games as an interactive entertainment medium are still comparatively new in regards to how they work and how they're distributed, from physical media to now digital downloads. Games used to be a clear product and the product was yours as soon as you purchased it, if I were to buy a game and wanted to use it after 20 years I still could. Now if I want to play a game, even a game for which I have a physical medium such as a DVD, it's not sure if I'm able to actually play the game or not depending on whether the developer has implemented certain features which stop the continued use of the game. When I purchase a game, whether it be a single player game or a multi player game, it isn't clear if the game requires always-online features, or if those features will be supported when I want to play my game. If a single player game requires me to log into a server that means the lifetime of the server dictates whether or not the game survives regardless of if the game has any online features at all. A game can boast anti-cheat or anti-pirating software which in turn requires the game to verify it with a server which may or may not be operational. As a step towards more sustainable practices for the gaming industry and gamers steps need to be taken in order to make sure that developers and publishers make games more transparent with their plans for the future that you make make a better informed choice when purchasing the games. A secondary issue is in regards to the handling of digital devices such as phones, home digital technology etc. that requires an internet connection. If I've purchased a product such as a TV any and all secondary services related to the product that are not required for the base function of the product should not be allowed to stop the base function of the product or force me to pay or give my personal information to a third party. If I buy a TV any and all smart features are secondary to the main feature of being able to use it to display content of my choice, be it via HDMI, DisplayPort, Firewire, messenger pigeon, or two cans and a string. This applies to all products that are possible to purchase for your home.
